MEMORANDUM OF POINTS AND AUTHORITIES IN SUPPORT OF EX PARTE MOTION FOR LEAVE TO FILE SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT (TRANSACTION ID # 100006453) FILED BY PLAINTIFF MAYERI, JOSEPH

LAW AND MOTION 302, THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. AS TO DEFENDANTS ANNETTA ROSENSTEIN AND PACIFIC UNION INTERNATIONAL INC.'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT, PLAINTIFF'S EX PARTE APPLICATION FOR LEAVE TO FILE A SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT IS GRANTED AND THUS DEFENDANTS' DEMURRER AND MOTION TO STRIKE ARE TAKEN OFF CALENDAR. BECAUSE DEFENDANTS WILL HAVE AN OPPORTUNITY TO FILE A DEMURRER AND MOTION TO STRIKE TO THE SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT, IF THEY DEEM IT APPROPRIATE TO DO SO, THERE IS NO LEGAL PREJUDICE TO THEM IN GRANTING LEAVE TO AMEND AND DOING SO FURTHER THE STRONG PUBLIC POLICY OF DECIDING CASES ON THEIR MERITS. ORDER RE: LEAVE TO FILE SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT SIGNED. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; CLERK: SEAN KANE; REPORTER: MELANIE GHENO, CSR #7489 (302/HEK)

LAW AND MOTION 302, THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. AS TO DEFENDANTS ANNETTA ROSENSTEIN AND PACIFIC UNION INTERNATIONAL INC.'S MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT, PLAINTIFF'S EX PARTE APPLICATION FOR LEAVE TO FILE A SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT IS GRANTED AND THUS DEFENDANTS' DEMURRER AND MOTION TO STRIKE ARE TAKEN OFF CALENDAR. BECAUSE DEFENDANTS WILL HAVE AN OPPORTUNITY TO FILE A DEMURRER AND MOTION TO STRIKE TO THE SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT, IF THEY DEEM IT APPROPRIATE TO DO SO, THERE IS NO LEGAL PREJUDICE TO THEM IN GRANTING LEAVE TO AMEND AND DOING SO FURTHER THE STRONG PUBLIC POLICY OF DECIDING CASES ON THEIR MERITS. Off Calendar Mar-04-2016 Continued To May-25-16 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Feb-05-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES: Atty. WADE S. CHURCH, ESQ. of Law Offices of John Iaccarino dba Iaccarino & Church, LLP, Tel No. 650-548-2600 appearing for the Plaintiff;
Atty. KENDRA J. PAPPAS, ESQ. of Shannon B. Jones Law Group, Tel No. 925-837-2317 appearing for Defendants Annetta Rosenstein and Pacific Union International Inc.

Matter argued and submitted by counsel. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. DEFENDANTS ANNETTA ROSENSTEIN AND PACIFIC UNION INTERNATIONAL INC.'S DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT is sustained with twenty days leave to amend as to the third cause of action for negligent misrepresentation and the fifth cause of action for fraud and overruled as to all other causes of action. The negligent misrepresentation and fraud causes of action are not alleged with the required specificity and it cannot be determined whether the alleged wrongdoing relates to future events, which would not be the basis for cognizable claims, or current or past legally viable claims for negligent misrepresentation and fraud. The remaining claims for breach of fiduciary duty, breach of contract, negligent supervision and breach of loyalty are adequately facts, which might be. Plaintiff is given one last opportunity to allege, if he can do sin good faith, pled. Paragraph 8 of the parties' agreement, coupled with paragraph 17 of the Second Amended Complaint, supports the breach of fiduciary duty, breach of contract and breach of loyalty claims. Case law recognizes breach of loyalty as a distinct cause of action. (Huong Que, Inc. v. Luu (2007) 150 Cal.App.4th 400, 410). The negligent supervision claim is a negligence claim that is not based on the cited Business and Professions Code 10159.2. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976 (302/HK)

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Oct-13-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. As to DEFENDANTS ANNETTA ROSENSTEIN AND PACIFIC UNION INTERNATIONAL INC.'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT, Plaintiff's ex parte application for leave to file a second amended complaint is granted and thus defendants' demurrer and motion to strike are taken off calendar. Because defendants will have an opportunity to file a demurrer and motion to strike to the second amended complaint, if they deem it appropriate to do so, there is no legal prejudice to them in granting leave to amend and doing so further the strong public policy of deciding cases on their merits. ORDER RE: LEAVE TO FILE SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT SIGNED. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Melanie Gheno, CSR #7489 (302/HEK)
